description_long: This bird has a slender, elongated body with a long, slightly downward-curved beak, ideal for probing into flowers or crevices. Its plumage is mostly a muted brown with subtle mottling, blending well with natural surroundings. The wings are compact and streamlined, while the tail is narrow and tapers to a point, with a slight gradation in feather length. The legs are short and not prominently visible, suggesting a perching or hovering lifestyle. The beak is a prominent feature, extending well beyond the head and giving the bird a distinctive profile. The overall appearance is reminiscent of nectar-feeding birds commonly found in urban parks and gardens.
description: A slender, brown bird with a long, curved beak and a pointed, graduated tail. Its muted plumage and streamlined body suggest a nectar-feeding species.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's long, curved beak and streamlined body with a pointed tail.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the narrow, tapered tail and subtle gradation in feather length.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the elongated beak and smooth body contour.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the beak's length and the bird's narrow head profile.
attribute_plumage_pattern: mottled brown with subtle gradients and minimal patterning
